analysis.problem: Inadequate representation of women in peace negotiations and governance institutions
analysis.root_cause: Systemic gender bias and lack of inclusive decision-making processes

proposed_actions.action: Implement quotas for women's representation in peace negotiations and governance institutions
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase women's representation in peace negotiations by 30% within the next 2 years

proposed_actions.action: Develop and implement training programs for policymakers and diplomats on gender-sensitive decision-making
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Train at least 50% of UN staff and national policymakers on gender-sensitive decision-making within the next year

evidence: Women's Role in Peace Processes More Urgent than Ever as Violent Conflicts Multiply, Rage across Globe, Speakers Tell Security Council
The Security Council's 10 non-permanent members requested a meeting on the Middle East and the Palestinian question, but no mention of women's representation
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of civilization, human dignity, and the biosphere, as inadequate representation of women in governance institutions can perpetuate inequality, social unrest, and environmental degradation
analysis.summary: The inadequate representation of women in peace negotiations and governance institutions is a pressing issue that requires immediate attention and action to address systemic gender bias and promote inclusive decision-making processes.

raw_items.title: Secretary-General Deeply Concerned over Escalation of Fighting in Sudan, Calls for Restraint
raw_items.summary: The following statement was issued today by the Spokesman for UN Secretary-General António Guterres:

raw_items.title: Security Council, 10177th Meeting (PM) Middle East
raw_items.summary: At 3 p.m., the Security Council will hear a briefing on the Middle East and the Palestinian question. The Council's 10 non-permanent members requested the meeting, with support from four of the five permanent members -- China, France, Russian Federation and the United Kingdom -- and a focus on the humanitarian situation in Gaza. Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher and a representative of an international non-governmental organization are

raw_items.title: Security Council, 10176th Meeting (AM) Libya
raw_items.summary: At 10 a.m., the Council hold its 60-day briefing on the situation in Libya. Special Representative of the Secretary-General and Head of the UN Support Mission in Libya (UNSMIL) Hanna Serwaa Tetteh will brief on recent political, security and humanitarian developments in the country.

raw_items.title: Women’s Role in Peace Processes More Urgent than Ever as Violent Conflicts Multiply, Rage across Globe, Speakers Tell Security Council
raw_items.summary: As conflicts multiply and women remain largely absent from peace negotiations, senior United Nations officials, civil society and Member States today urged the Security Council to move beyond rhetoric and make women’s participation in peace processes funded and non-negotiable, warning that peace built without women is “only a pause in violence”.
